topic: What is the impact of quantum computing on encoded passwords?
report:
# The Impact of Quantum Computers on Passwords and Passkeys
## Introduction
The emergence of quantum computers has raised concerns about the security of traditional encryption systems and passwords. As quantum computers become more capable, they are likely to compromise most of today's encryption systems relying on traditional asymmetric encryption [Source 1]. This report aims to explore the impact of quantum computers on passwords and passkeys, and whether passkeys will remain a secure form of authentication in a world with quantum computing.

## Key Findings
Quantum computers can break certain types of encryption and hashing algorithms currently used to secure password storage [Source 5]. The main concern is that quantum computers would be able to crack commonly used asymmetric encryption algorithms such as RSA [Source 3]. However, passkeys, a new form of passwordless authentication, use a pair of keys: a public key and a private key, with the private key never leaving the user's vault unless securely shared [Source 6]. This design is based on public key cryptography and differs from traditional username-and-password credentials [Source 2]. While some have questioned the longevity of passkey technology in a world with quantum computing, the current understanding is that passkeys are among the few inherently secure login methods around [Source 2].

## Conclusion
The impact of quantum computers on passwords is a significant concern, as they can break certain types of encryption and hashing algorithms. However, passkeys, with their use of public and private keys, may remain a secure form of authentication. Further research is needed to fully understand the implications of quantum computing on passkey technology. Currently, the information available suggests that passkeys are designed to be secure, but the long-term effects of quantum computing on this technology are still being explored [Source 8].

## Sources
[Source 1] https://blog.knowbe4.com/how-does-quantum-impact-passwords
[Source 2] https://1password.com/blog/passkeys-quantum-computers-encryption
[Source 3] https://www.reddit.com/r/computerscience/comments/1md6c6n/quantum_computing_only_concerns_about_brute/
[Source 4] https://1password.com/blog/passkeys-quantum-computers-encryption
[Source 5] https://specopssoft.com/blog/post-quantum-cryptography-passwords/
